scope.modelIdentity: runtime-declared GPT-5 family; exact model build not exposed to this repository baseline
scope.reasoningLevel: not observable from the local metadata probes
scope.loader: current Codex session exposes Skill metadata and loads selected Skill bodies; this artifact does not claim deterministic implicit activation
scope.permissions: workspace write limited to the bound repository and temporary workspace; local capability inspection was read-only
scope.accountBoundary: no account connection, OAuth, credential read, private remote, or external write

skillNameSurface.interpretation: Directory-name overlap proves enumeration and collision-management demand; it does not prove that every root is active, that same-name bodies conflict, or that any duplicate should be removed.

hookMetadata.interpretation: Local candidate policy and host-profile metadata exist, but activation, event delivery, failure behavior, value delta, and cost are unverified.

demandBaselines.insufficiency: No current evidence proves a fresh carrier-neutral active-state contract, re-entry handshake, no-delta semantics, or equivalent behavior across hosts and weaker agents.
demandBaselines.externalMetadataQuestion: Which maintained public Skills or non-Skill state/continuation patterns explicitly cover authority-bounded active state, re-entry, freshness, and honest phase exit without becoming a persistence engine?

demandBaselines.insufficiency: The local inventory exposes substantial overlap, while active-root precedence, implicit activation, collision behavior, Hook effectiveness, cross-host parity, and maintenance cost remain unverified.
demandBaselines.externalMetadataQuestion: Which maintained public Skills, managers, registries, or non-Skill governance patterns reduce enumeration and collision burden while preserving native-first routing, provenance, trust, fallback, and lifecycle evidence?

demandBaselines.insufficiency: No Skill or general runtime capability can supply missing legal, professional, safety, privacy, security, medical, financial, production, organizational, or affected-subject authority.
Current evidence does not provide a complete source-to-consumer projection graph, live discovery state, cross-host compatibility, renewal/deprecation behavior, or public-private edge verification.
demandBaselines.externalMetadataQuestion: Which maintained public resource-governance, Skill-registry, provenance, synchronization, or lifecycle patterns provide source-to-consumer edge evidence without claiming runtime or standards authority?

baselineDecision.nextGate: bounded public metadata discovery, clustering, and source-pinned non-executing representative review for the three eligible demand records

limitations: The baseline is one current Codex desktop and local-filesystem observation; it is not a Claude, TRAE, WorkBuddy, Copilot, Grok, ChatGPT, or universal Agent result.
File presence, metadata, path resolution, and hash equality do not prove loader precedence, implicit invocation, instruction compliance, runtime health, behavioral equivalence, or value.
No Hook, Skill, Plugin, App, MCP server, external candidate, account, or remote was executed, enabled, connected, installed, or mutated.
The 422-file snapshot includes plugin cache and multiple roots; it is not a curated inventory and does not authorize deletion or consolidation.
Public discovery can compare metadata and source text only; it cannot establish residual gaps or adoption without later behavior, alternative, cost, security, license, and owner review.
recheckTriggers: Codex CLI, desktop package, model, reasoning, loader, permission, workspace, or root configuration changes
Skill or Hook files, hashes, policies, host profiles, activation state, or runtime observability changes
a different host or account becomes the target of a bounded comparison
candidate metadata suggests an existing path closes or materially changes an insufficiency
a residual-gap, Hook, adaptation, repository-authoring, standard, or consumer-projection decision is proposed
